column1: Alienation [AL1: Submitting AI-assisted work makes me feel less proud of my learning achievements]
column2: Alienation [AL2: I worry that AI-generated assignments donot truly reflect what I've learned.]
column3: Alienation [AL3: Using AI for coursework makes learning feel less intellectually rewarding.]
column4: Alienation [AL5: When AI can write essays or solve problems, I question why I need to learn these skills.]
column5: Alienation [AL6: AI capabilities make my own academic growth feel less valuable.]
column6: Alienation [AL7: AI tools reduce meaningful feedback from my teachers.]
column7: Alienation [AL8: Using AI for group work makes collaboration feel less authentic.]
column8: Communication [Comm2: I feel confident when explaining complex topics to others in class discussions or presentations.]
column9: Collaboration [Collab1: I prefer working alone with AI tools rather than discussing ideas with peers.]
column10: Collaboration [Collab3: Using AI tools has made me less likely to seek help from classmates.]
column11: Creative Thinking [Creat1: I use AI tools even when I could think of original ideas myself.]
column12: Creative Thinking [Creat3: My assignments are less original because I adapt AI-generated ideas instead of developing my own.]
column13: Problem-Solving [Prob1: When facing a problem, I go to AI tools before trying to solve it myself.]
column14: Problem-Solving [Prob2: I have become less persistent in solving academic challenges because I know AI will help.]
column15: Problem-Solving [Prob3: I often skip brainstorming or planning because I expect AI to do most of the work.]
column16: Self-Confidence [SC1: I doubt my ability to complete tasks without AI support.]
column17: Self-Confidence [SC3: The more I use AI, the less confident I feel doing academic work on my own.]
